title: Daily Briefing — Apr 17
headline: Tech leads the charge, but rotation signals say 'keep your head on a swivel.'
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: SPY’s holding $701.66, hugging that 20 MA at $664.01 like it's home base. The VIX has fallen back to 17.88, well below its 20-day, which means the market's caught a breather but isn’t in full ‘risk on’ euphoria. Risk appetite has rebounded—Fear & Greed is 63.3 (Greed)—but we're not at the contrarian red zone just yet.

XLK (tech) and XLC (communication) are leading both in price and RRG momentum, with heavyweights like NVDA, MSFT, and GOOGL pushing new highs above their 20s and 50s. But keep an eye on sector divergence: energy (XLE), industrials (XLI), and materials (XLB) are rolling over, all in weakening or elevated risk regimes—classic rotation out of cyclicals into growth. Financials (XLF, $52.03) are improving, but still lagging their major moving averages. That’s not bullish confirmation for the whole economy.

Contrarian hat on: the last time Greed climbed this fast off a Fear low was just before several 5–10% tech pullbacks—and we're seeing leaders extended well above their 20s (NVDA $18 above, MSFT $42 above). Retail FOMO is starting to show, so while trend is your friend, don’t chase—abundance mindset. No major breakdowns, but if bonds (HYG/LQD) start to slip, or if XLF rolls over, be ready to reduce risk. Less trades = more wins.
callout_body:
SPY: $664.01 (20 MA), $673.36 (50 MA) — mean reversion zones
NVDA: $180.28 (20 MA) — watch for snap-backs if stretched
XLF: $52.29 (200 MA resistance)
XLK: $138.67 (20 MA support)
VIX: Stay under 20, but first volatility spike warns of regime shift
HYG: $79.48 (20 MA) — early warning for credit risk
action_items: Trim any tech laggards extended >10% above their 20 MAs; don’t chase breakouts here.
Watch XLF and HYG/LQD closely—if either breaks below their 20 MAs, shift defensive per rules.
closing_quote: Remember: If it's in the press, it's already in the price. Follow the flows.
